overview: Sultai Tempo in Legacy leverages cheap, efficient removal and card advantage to control the board while racing with aggressive threats. By cycling through Ponder and Brainstorm, the pilot finds answers to early pressure and sets up lethal combinations. The deck aims to stabilize quickly using Fatal Push and other spot removal, then close out games with resilient creatures and value engines.
win_conditions: Establishing board presence with efficient creatures while denying opponent's threats
Cycling into lethal combinations or value engines through consistent draw spells
Outlasting opponents through superior card advantage and cheap removal

keep_criteria: Hands with one or two lands and a removal spell like Fatal Push
Draw spells that can find missing pieces such as Ponder or Brainstorm
Early threats that apply pressure while setting up answers
mulligan_criteria: Mana screws with three or more lands and no interaction
Hands lacking removal or card draw in the first three turns
Slow value cards without early board presence or answers

early_game: Play lands efficiently and cast Fatal Push to remove key early threats
Use Brainstorm and Ponder to dig for answers and smooth out mana
Establish a small creature to apply pressure while protecting it with removal
mid_game: Transition to larger threats and value cards while maintaining board control
Trade efficiently and cycle through draw spells to find game-ending pieces
Look for opportunities to swing for lethal while keeping removal in hand
late_game: Leverage card advantage to outvalue opponents and find finishers
Use targeted removal to clear the path for lethal swings
Stabilize against combo decks by disrupting their key pieces and racing with resilient creatures

key_cards.name: Ponder
key_cards.notes: Cast on turn one to find lands or answers, then replay later to set up combos or find removal.

key_cards.name: Brainstorm
key_cards.notes: Use to dig for Fatal Push or early threats, discarding unneeded lands or slow cards to maintain tempo.

key_cards.name: Fatal Push
key_cards.notes: Target opponent's key creatures or your own blockers to maintain board control and protect your game plan.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize casting Fatal Push before playing creatures to avoid trading into removal
Use Ponder to find lands when behind on mana, and Brainstorm to accelerate when ahead
Hold up removal for opponent's key threats rather than casting it preemptively unless necessary

matchups.advice: Focus on early Fatal Push and board control to stall until you can stabilize with larger threats.
Apply early pressure with creatures while cycling through draw spells to find answers to their late-game threats.
Disrupt their key pieces with removal and use Ponder and Brainstorm to find interaction before they assemble their win condition.

common_threats: Fast aggro creatures that pressure early
Counterspells that disrupt your game plan
Board wipes that clear your board presence
